title: Pi-first doctrine
goal: Record the Pi-first doctrine so future design work treats Pi as the sole real worker harness, daemon state as durable JSON truth, and fake as a deterministic test seam rather than a portability layer.
acceptance: VISION.md states Pi-native worker execution, harness-neutral daemon state, and fake-as-test-seam without implying future multi-harness worker support.
docs/workflow-invariants.md records product decisions D1-D6, including the Pi documented-surface coupling rule and ambient-feedback doctrine.
The three standing rejections are recorded with rationale: Pi acceptance gates, fallbackModels silent failover, and auto-login or credential mutation.
README.md positions Khazad-Doom as a project-level workflow daemon for Pi, complementary to session-scoped delegation tools such as pi-subagents.
No doc, prompt, or CLI-facing wording claims harness-agnostic or multi-harness worker execution; harness-neutral daemon state/CLI status wording remains allowed.
must_ask_if: The implementation would remove or weaken the fake runner as the deterministic test seam.
A document would claim support for a second real worker harness or Pi-side acceptance gates.
verify: bash -lc '! rg -ni "harness-agnostic|harness agnostic" README.md .pi/memory docs/workflow-invariants.md skills/khazad-doom/SKILL.md src'
